clusters.title: Who holds the cards at the Trump-Xi summit in Beijing?
clusters.neutral_headline: China’s Xi expected to press Trump on Taiwan, tariffs during summit
bullet_summary: US President Donald Trump said he would ask Xi Jinping to "open up" China to American firms as he headed to Beijing on Wednesday for a high-stakes summit that will also bring up the Iran war
Trump has said he will have a "long talk" about Iran with Chinese leader Xi Jinping and will also discuss US arms sales to Taiwan

items.title: Who holds the cards at the Trump-Xi summit in Beijing?
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump arrived in Beijing on Wednesday for his first visit to China since 2017. The stakes are high for the two-day summit, which analysts suggest could mark a tipping point in the relationship between the superpowers – likely in Chinese President Xi Jinping’s favour.
items.content: US President Donald Trump arrived in Beijing on Wednesday for his first visit to China since 2017. The stakes are high for the two-day summit, which analysts suggest could mark a tipping point in the relationship between the superpowers – likely in Chinese President Xi Jinping’s favour.

items.title: Ahead of Trump-Xi summit, China warns on US arms sales to Taiwan
items.standfirst: China reiterated its strong opposition to US arms sales to Taiwan on Wednesday, calling on Washington to honour its commitments ahead of US President Donald Trump's arrival for a summit in Beijing. The issue of democratically governed Taiwan, which China views as its own territory, ​and weapons sales ‌to Taipei is certain to be discussed during two days of meetings this week between Trump and ⁠Chinese President Xi Jinping. FRANCE 24's Yena Lee reports from Beijing, China.
items.content: China reiterated its strong opposition to US arms sales to Taiwan on Wednesday, calling on Washington to honour its commitments ahead of US President Donald Trump's arrival for a summit in Beijing. The issue of democratically governed Taiwan, which China views as its own territory, ​and weapons sales ‌to Taipei is certain to be discussed during two days of meetings this week between Trump and ⁠Chinese President Xi Jinping. FRANCE 24's Yena Lee reports from Beijing, China.

items.title: Trump vows to push Xi to 'open up' China at superpower summit
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump said he would ask Xi Jinping to "open up" China to American firms as he headed to Beijing on Wednesday for a high-stakes summit that will also bring up the Iran war. "I will be asking President Xi, a Leader of extraordinary distinction, to 'open up' China so that these brilliant people can work their magic, and help bring the People's Republic to an even higher level!" Trump wrote on social media after departing Washington.
items.content: US President Donald Trump said he would ask Xi Jinping to "open up" China to American firms as he headed to Beijing on Wednesday for a high-stakes summit that will also bring up the Iran war. "I will be asking President Xi, a Leader of extraordinary distinction, to 'open up' China so that these brilliant people can work their magic, and help bring the People's Republic to an even higher level!" Trump wrote on social media after departing Washington.

items.title: Trump heads to China, says no need for Xi's help on Iran war
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump ​headed to China on Wednesday for a high-stakes summit with President Xi Jinping, saying he does not expect to need Beijing's help to end the war with Iran and ease Tehran's grip on the Strait of Hormuz. Speaking before departing Washington, Trump played down the role China could have in resolving the conflict, which has continued to block maritime traffic through a waterway ​that normally carries one-fifth of ‌the world's oil supply. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson reports from Washington DC.
items.content: US President Donald Trump ​headed to China on Wednesday for a high-stakes summit with President Xi Jinping, saying he does not expect to need Beijing's help to end the war with Iran and ease Tehran's grip on the Strait of Hormuz. Speaking before departing Washington, Trump played down the role China could have in resolving the conflict, which has continued to block maritime traffic through a waterway ​that normally carries one-fifth of ‌the world's oil supply. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson reports from Washington DC.

items.title: Trump heads to China for high-stakes meeting with Xi
items.standfirst: Trade, Taiwan and tensions with Iran are surefire topics for President Trump's meeting with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.
items.content: Trade, Taiwan and tensions with Iran are surefire topics for President Trump's meeting with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

items.title: Trump set to meet with Xi in Beijing as war and inflation weigh on his presidency
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump is set to arrive in Beijing on Wednesday for his highly anticipated summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping at a restless moment for a world worried about war, trade and artificial intelligence. While Trump likes to project a sense of strength, the visit occurs at a delicate moment for his presidency as his popularity at home has been weighed down by the US and Israel's war with Iran and rising inflation as a consequence of that conflict. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Commentator Douglas Herbert tells us more.
items.content: US President Donald Trump is set to arrive in Beijing on Wednesday for his highly anticipated summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping at a restless moment for a world worried about war, trade and artificial intelligence. While Trump likes to project a sense of strength, the visit occurs at a delicate moment for his presidency as his popularity at home has been weighed down by the US and Israel's war with Iran and rising inflation as a consequence of that conflict. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Commentator Douglas Herbert tells us more.

items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump's highly anticipated visit to Beijing on Wednesday comes at a restless moment for a world worried about war and trade disputes. Trump has said he will have a "long talk" about Iran with Chinese leader Xi Jinping and will also discuss US arms sales to Taiwan. Here's what to know about the summit.

items.standfirst: President Donald Trump will begin a two-day summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing on Thursday as the two countries engage in a fierce trade war and the U.S. conflict with Iran rages on.
items.content: President Donald Trump will begin a two-day summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing on Thursday as the two countries engage in a fierce trade war and the U.S. conflict with Iran rages on.

items.title: Trump heads to high-stakes summit in China

clusters.title: Jason Collins, first openly gay active NBA player, dies after cancer battle
clusters.neutral_headline: Jason Collins, first openly gay active NBA player, dies after cancer battle
bullet_summary: Jason Collins, the NBA's first openly gay player, who went on to become a pioneer for inclusion and an ambassador for the league, has died, his family announced Tuesday
Reported by Al Jazeera, BBC World, CBS News

items.title: Jason Collins, first openly gay active NBA player, dies after cancer battle
items.standfirst: The 213cm centre, who publicly came out as gay in 2013, had been battling a brain tumour.
items.content: The 213cm centre, who publicly came out as gay in 2013, had been battling a brain tumour.

clusters.title: France confines more than 1,700 people to cruise ship after suspected norovirus death
clusters.neutral_headline: France confines more than 1,700 on cruise ship after suspected norovirus death
bullet_summary: One 90-year-old passenger had died and about 50 people had shown symptoms of the virus, French health officials said
Ambassador Cruise Line also confirmed that a 92-year-old man died on board earlier this week, but he did not report any symptoms at the time and the cause of his death is yet to be established

items.title: France confines more than 1,700 people to cruise ship after suspected norovirus death
items.standfirst: One elderly passenger on vessel docked in Bordeaux has died and about 50 people have symptoms, say officialsFrench authorities have confined more than 1,700 passengers and crew members to a cruise ship docked in Bordeaux after a passenger died from suspected norovirus, officials have said.The vessel Ambition, which is carrying 1,233 passengers, most of them British or Irish nationals, arrived in Bordeaux on Tuesday. One 90-year-old passenger had died and about 50 people had shown symptoms of the virus, French health officials said. Continue reading...
items.content: One elderly passenger on vessel docked in Bordeaux has died and about 50 people have symptoms, say officialsFrench authorities have confined more than 1,700 passengers and crew members to a cruise ship docked in Bordeaux after a passenger died from suspected norovirus, officials have said.The vessel Ambition, which is carrying 1,233 passengers, most of them British or Irish nationals, arrived in Bordeaux on Tuesday. One 90-year-old passenger had died and about 50 people had shown symptoms of the virus, French health officials said. Continue reading...

items.title: More than 1,700 confined on cruise ship in Bordeaux after suspected norovirus death – Europe live
items.standfirst: Operator says majority of passengers on board of cruise ship stuck are British, while about 50 people are showing symptomsResponding to the Guardian’s questions, the operator also confirmed that the vast majority of the 1,187 guests on board are British. There are also 514 crew members.Ambassador Cruise Line also confirmed that a 92-year-old man died on board earlier this week, but he did not report any symptoms at the time and the cause of his death is yet to be established. Continue reading...

items.title: France confines more than 1,700 on cruise ship after suspected norovirus death
items.standfirst: More than 1,700 passengers and crew have been confined on a cruise ship docked in the French city of Bordeaux after a 90-year-old passenger died from a suspected case of norovirus, a highly contagious form of gastroenteritis.
items.content: More than 1,700 passengers and crew have been confined on a cruise ship docked in the French city of Bordeaux after a 90-year-old passenger died from a suspected case of norovirus, a highly contagious form of gastroenteritis.

clusters.title: Tens of thousands demonstrate in Argentina over Milei university cuts
clusters.neutral_headline: Tens of thousands protest in Argentina over Milei university cuts

items.title: Tens of thousands demonstrate in Argentina over Milei university cuts
items.standfirst: Tens of thousands of Argentines took to the streets Tuesday in Buenos Aires to protest libertarian President Javier Milei's deep cuts to the budgets of public universities. The fourth major demonstration over university budgets since Milei took office in December 2023 came a day after it emerged that his government is planning further cuts to education and health spending.
items.content: Tens of thousands of Argentines took to the streets Tuesday in Buenos Aires to protest libertarian President Javier Milei's deep cuts to the budgets of public universities. The fourth major demonstration over university budgets since Milei took office in December 2023 came a day after it emerged that his government is planning further cuts to education and health spending.

items.title: Tens of thousands protest in Argentina over Milei university cuts
items.standfirst: Argentines protest Javier Milei’s deep cuts to tuition-free universities as staff wages plunge and funding law stalls.
items.content: Argentines protest Javier Milei’s deep cuts to tuition-free universities as staff wages plunge and funding law stalls.

clusters.title: Japanese snack packages turning black-and-white as Iran war depletes ink supply
clusters.neutral_headline: Japanese snack packages turning black-and-white as Iran war depletes ink supply
bullet_summary: Calbee, whose potato chip brands in particular are known for brightly coloured bag designs, said 14 of its products would switch to monochrome branding by the end of May
Reported by NPR News, Guardian Business

items.title: Japanese snack packages turning black-and-white as Iran war depletes ink supply
items.standfirst: The packaging on some snacks is turning black-and-white, as the war in Iran disrupts the supply of an ingredient used in colored ink. Calbee's chips originally came in a bright-orange bag.
items.content: The packaging on some snacks is turning black-and-white, as the war in Iran disrupts the supply of an ingredient used in colored ink. Calbee's chips originally came in a bright-orange bag.(Image credit: AP)

items.title: Iran war oil shortage forces Japan snack giant to use black-and-white packaging
items.standfirst: Calbee to switch its brightly coloured packaging to black and white because war has disrupted supply of certain raw materials used in inkJapan’s biggest snack maker has been forced to use black-and-white packaging for some flagship products because of ink ingredient shortages caused by the strait of Hormuz blockade.Calbee, whose potato chip brands in particular are known for brightly coloured bag designs, said 14 of its products would switch to monochrome branding by the end of May. Continue reading...

clusters.title: France intensifies hantavirus surveillance as 22 contact cases identified
clusters.neutral_headline: France intensifies hantavirus surveillance as 22 contact cases identified
bullet_summary: The hantavirus outbreak has now reached 11 total reported cases, 9 of which have been confirmed
In France, a woman infected in the deadly virus outbreak on a cruise ship is critically ill, a doctor at the Paris hospital caring for the sickened passenger said Tuesday
The outbreak has now reached 11 total reported cases, 9 of which have been confirmed

items.title: France intensifies hantavirus surveillance as 22 contact cases identified
items.standfirst: The hantavirus outbreak has now reached 11 total reported cases, 9 of which have been confirmed. In France, a woman infected in the deadly virus outbreak on a cruise ship is critically ill, a doctor at the Paris hospital caring for the sickened passenger said Tuesday. With 22 contact cases in the country overall, authorities say there is no widespread transmission of the virus at this moment.
items.content: The hantavirus outbreak has now reached 11 total reported cases, 9 of which have been confirmed. In France, a woman infected in the deadly virus outbreak on a cruise ship is critically ill, a doctor at the Paris hospital caring for the sickened passenger said Tuesday. With 22 contact cases in the country overall, authorities say there is no widespread transmission of the virus at this moment.

items.title: French hantavirus patient is critically ill as outbreak grows to 11
items.standfirst: A French woman infected in the deadly hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship is critically ill and being treated with an artificial lung. The outbreak has now reached 11 total reported cases, 9 of which have been confirmed.
items.content: A French woman infected in the deadly hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship is critically ill and being treated with an artificial lung. The outbreak has now reached 11 total reported cases, 9 of which have been confirmed.(Image credit: Michel Euler)
